summary: Backend API testing for MapForge complete. All 16 tests passed covering auth (register/login/me cookie+bearer/logout), maps CRUD (ownership enforcement, partial update with layers/polygons/markers/base64 background), share toggle, and public map access. No _id leakage found in any response.
critical_code_review_comments: CORS: backend/.env has CORS_ORIGINS="*" but server.py uses only FRONTEND_URL (single origin). The CORS_ORIGINS variable is dead/unused — consider removing or wiring it.
FastAPI on_event('startup'/'shutdown') is deprecated in modern FastAPI; consider migrating to lifespan context manager.
Login endpoint has no brute-force lockout/rate-limiting (auth_testing.md guideline mentions lockout after 5 fails). Not implemented; may be acceptable for this MVP but worth flagging.

PUT /api/maps/{id} accepts background_image as base64 data URL with no size validation — could allow very large documents (MongoDB 16MB limit risk). Recommend max-length / store in GridFS or object storage.
ACCESS_TOKEN_MINUTES = 7 days is long-lived without refresh token rotation; acceptable for MVP but document the tradeoff.
Cookie is set with samesite='none' + secure=True — correct for cross-site preview deployment; just note it requires HTTPS (which preview URL satisfies).
test_credentials: Used dynamically-generated TEST_<uuid>@example.com / test1234 users for isolation. /app/memory/test_credentials.md present.
seed_data_creation: Each test run creates fresh TEST_-prefixed users and one map; the map is deleted by the final test. Stale TEST_ users remain in DB (no cleanup endpoint).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_backend_api.py uses requests + module-scoped fixtures (user_ctx, created_map). It validates cookie-based and Bearer auth, ownership-based 404s, share toggle round-trip, and public map endpoint. Run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_backend_api.py -v. Backend uses bcrypt + PyJWT HS256, cookies httpOnly/secure/samesite=none. Frontend testing was explicitly skipped per main agent request.
